steps.title: Set up the weight-loss equation
steps.content:
The bear starts at 338 pounds and loses 0.7 pounds per day. After $d$ days, the weight is the initial weight minus the total loss:
$$310 = 338 - 0.7d$$

steps.title: Solve for the number of days
steps.content:
Rearrange to isolate $0.7d$:
$$0.7d = 338 - 310 = 28$$
Divide both sides by 0.7:
$$d = \frac{28}{0.7} = 40$$
The bear weighed 310 pounds after 40 days.
